title: Campylospermum borneense
taxonomy: Campylospermum borneense is classified within the family Ochnaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the ironwood family. It belongs to the genus Campylospermum, which comprises several species of trees and shrubs native to tropical regions. The family Ochnaceae is an ancient group of plants that has remained largely unchanged over millions of years. Members of this family are characterized by their unique flowers and seeds, which are often protected by a hard, woody covering. Campylospermum borneense is one of the many species within the Ochnaceae family that is adapted to life in tropical rainforests.

intro: Campylospermum borneense is a plant species native to Borneo, belonging to the family Ochnaceae, commonly known as the chaff-tree family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities that may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Campylospermum borneense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
